title: pilosella brachiata subsp. nematocaulon
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. nematocaulon is a rare subspecies of the plant Pilosella brachiata, also known as wood sorrel, which is a member of the dandelion family and has a unique ability to photosynthesize through its leaves. It has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pilosella brachiata subsp. nematocaulon include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having potential antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Pilosella brachiata subsp. nematocaulon include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
